highlights: ComfyUI provides a node-based interface for generative AI that offers professional creators granular control and reproducibility, moving away from the unpredictable "slot machine" nature of standard prompt boxes.

Anthropic has sparked industry debate by calling for a global slowdown in AI development to allow safety research to keep pace, leading critics to argue that developers should lead by example and pause their own models first.

Senator Bernie Sanders proposed the American AI Sovereign Wealth Fund Act, which suggests a one-time 50% tax on AI company stock to redistribute wealth created from the collective intelligence of humanity.

The 2028 presidential election is predicted to be defined by AI job displacement, focusing on whether the massive wealth generated by the technology benefits the general public or only a small group of Silicon Valley elites.

Research into Universal Basic Income suggests that a financial cushion does not decrease the desire to work, but instead allows individuals the flexibility to make better long-term career decisions and pursue professional growth.
summary:
This episode of *This Week in Startups*, hosted by Jason Calacanis and Lon Harris, features a deep dive into the evolving landscape of artificial intelligence, centered on a demo and interview with Yoland Yan, CEO of ComfyUI. Yan demonstrates the power of node-based interfaces for AI generation, which allow for granular, professional-grade control over image and video models like Ideogram and Stable Diffusion. The discussion highlights the strategic value of inference engines and the "operating system" layer of generative AI, while Calacanis offers tactical advice on scaling sales teams in lower-cost hubs and the importance of founder perseverance over returning capital during a pivot.

The transcript also explores the intensifying political friction surrounding AI’s rapid advancement. The hosts debate Anthropic’s controversial call for a global slowdown in AI development and Bernie Sanders’ proposed "American AI Sovereign Wealth Fund Act," which seeks to levy a 50% stock tax on AI companies to redistribute wealth created from "collective human intelligence." Calacanis predicts that the 2028 presidential election will be defined by AI-driven job displacement, leading to a "grand bargain" involving Universal Basic Income (UBI) and the consolidation of inefficient social welfare programs into direct cash payments.

The show concludes with a look at AI's integration into specific industries and the broader creator economy. The hosts discuss Brian Chesky’s new AI design lab at Airbnb and the challenges of "wrapper" startups in the travel sector. In the "Off-Duty" segment, they analyze the success of low-budget horror films made by YouTubers, such as *Obsession* and *Backrooms*, which have achieved massive ROI at the box office. Finally, they touch on the darker side of the gig economy with "pump.fun," a platform that allows users to set bounties for real-world tasks, raising concerns about the ethics of "dystopian" and dangerous stunts for digital rewards.

Anthropic wrote a blog post calling for a global AI slowdown. Meanwhile, Sen. Bernie Sanders wants the government to seize 50% of every major AI company’s stock.
